title: Mechanism of Rearrangement of Heterocyclic N-Oxides
author: Binod Kumar Varma
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The rearrangement of 2-methylpyridine- N -oxide 1  in acetic anhydride afforded a mixture of two esters (II and III), which, on.hydrolysis and fractional distillation, furnish. ed 2-hydroxymethylpyridine (I) and 2-methyl-6-hydroxypyridine (IV). The isolation of (IV) has been reported only by the Japanese group 2  and not by other workers 1,2,3
steps_raw: The rearrangement of 2-methylpyridine- N -oxide 1  in acetic anhydride afforded a mixture of two esters (II and III), which, on.hydrolysis and fractional distillation, furnish. ed 2-hydroxymethylpyridine (I) and 2-methyl-6-hydroxypyridine (IV). The isolation of (IV) has been reported only by the Japanese group 2  and not by other workers 1,2,3
